concept: Attach a bioreductive trigger (e.g. a 2-nitroimidazole) to a cytotoxic warhead; the trigger is reduced and fragments only in low-oxygen tissue, releasing the toxin there.
mechanism: Under tumor hypoxia, one-electron reductases reduce the 2-nitroimidazole; the radical fragments to release the cytotoxin (a bromo-isophosphoramide mustard). In normoxia the radical is back-oxidized, making release hypoxia-selective.

citation.label: Sun JD, Liu Q, Wang J, et al. Selective tumor hypoxia targeting by hypoxia-activated prodrug TH-302 inhibits tumor growth in preclinical models of cancer. Clin Cancer Res. 2012;18(3):758-70.
citation.note: Author/year verified via NCBI as Sun JD, 2012 (the Claude Science draft mislabeled it 'Duan 2011').
